code:
def fibonacci_search(arr, target):
    n = len(arr)

    # Initialize Fibonacci numbers
    # fib_m_minus_2 represents F(k-2)
    # fib_m_minus_1 represents F(k-1)
    # fib_m represents F(k)
    fib_m_minus_2 = 0  # F(0)
    fib_m_minus_1 = 1  # F(1)
    fib_m = fib_m_minus_1 + fib_m_minus_2  # F(2)

    # Find the smallest Fibonacci number F(k) that is greater than or equal to n
    while fib_m < n:
        fib_m_minus_2 = fib_m_minus_1
        fib_m_minus_1 = fib_m
        fib_m = fib_m_minus_1 + fib_m_minus_2

    # 'offset' helps in eliminating the searched part of the array.
    # It marks the beginning of the current search range.
    offset = -1

    # While there are elements to be checked (equivalent to fib_m_minus_1 > 0)
    # fib_m will eventually become 1 (representing F(1)) and fib_m_minus_1 will become 0 (representing F(0))
    # or fib_m will become 2 (representing F(2)) and fib_m_minus_1 will become 1 (representing F(1))
    while fib_m > 1:
        # Calculate the index to check
        # i is the index equivalent to (offset + F(k-2))
        # Ensure 'i' does not exceed the array bounds (n-1)
        i = min(offset + fib_m_minus_2, n - 1)

        # If the target is found at index i
        if arr[i] == target:
            return i
        # If the target is greater than the element at index i,
        # the target is in the right sub-array.
        # We discard the left part including arr[i].
        # The new problem size effectively becomes F(k-1).
        # We update Fibonacci numbers: F(k) -> F(k-1), F(k-1) -> F(k-2), F(k-2) -> F(k-3)
        elif arr[i] < target:
            fib_m = fib_m_minus_1
            fib_m_minus_1 = fib_m_minus_2
            fib_m_minus_2 = fib_m - fib_m_minus_1  # This is F(k-3)
            offset = i  # Update offset to start search from i+1
        # If the target is less than the element at index i,
        # the target is in the left sub-array.
        # We discard the right part including arr[i].
        # The new problem size effectively becomes F(k-2).
        # We update Fibonacci numbers: F(k) -> F(k-2), F(k-1) -> F(k-3), F(k-2) -> F(k-4)
        else:  # arr[i] > target
            fib_m = fib_m_minus_2
            fib_m_minus_1 = fib_m_minus_1 - fib_m_minus_2  # This is F(k-3)
            fib_m_minus_2 = fib_m - fib_m_minus_1  # This is F(k-4)
            # offset remains unchanged as the search space is to the left of i

    # After the loop, fib_m might be 1 or 0.
    # If fib_m_minus_1 is 1, it means one element potentially remains at offset + 1.
    if fib_m_minus_1 == 1 and offset + 1 < n and arr[offset + 1] == target:
        return offset + 1

    return -1
